_meta.title: CQB Ink Beats
_meta.description: Cover-test tiers + CQB outcome beats fired from ai.gd during station contact. Phase 3e.

cqb_cover_pass_clean.speaker: narrator
cqb_cover_pass_clean.text: The gate at {station} gave way without a second look. {captain.short_form} kept the invoice-code clean, the papers straight. Nobody asked questions. Your crew exhales at once.

cqb_cover_pass_rough.speaker: narrator
cqb_cover_pass_rough.text: The gate at {station} let you through — but the cameras caught every face. {captain.short_form} felt the weight of it in the corridor after. They'll remember this pass.

cqb_enter.speaker: narrator
cqb_enter.text: The gate at {station} doesn't negotiate. A {alien_archetype} steps from the cover with a {weapon_id} in hand. The grid is {grid_size} by {grid_size}. Your crew draws breath and opens fire.

cqb_end_won.speaker: narrator
cqb_end_won.text: The last alien falls. Your crew stands breathing in the wreckage of the gate. Nobody walks away clean — but everyone walks away. The bond between them holds.

cqb_end_lost.speaker: narrator
cqb_end_lost.text: The fight goes the wrong way. An ally cuts the line — pull back, abort, live to run another day. The station stays. The ledger records a loss.

cqb_end_fled.speaker: narrator
cqb_end_fled.text: You got out, but you left a trace. The {alien_archetype} will remember your ship. Next time, the gate will be harder. This run ends — but the belt doesn't forget.

cqb_cover_fail_caught.speaker: narrator
cqb_cover_fail_caught.text: The gate at {station} flagged you on sight. Your invoice-code matched a watch-list entry — real or fabricated, it doesn't matter now. The cameras lock on. Security steps out of the corridor. Fight or talk. Both cost something.

choices.label: Talk your way out. Play confused.

cqb_cover_fail_withdraw.speaker: narrator
cqb_cover_fail_withdraw.text: The gate scan turns red. Before security moves, {captain.short_form} keys the override and pulls the crew back through the airlock. You're clear — for now. But your face is in their system and the station is not.

cqb_flanked_warning.speaker: narrator
cqb_flanked_warning.text: The eng tech's voice crackles on the comm: movement on the side — two hostiles pressing the flank. Full cover on the left is gone. The grid just got smaller. Hold or fold.

cqb_fold_declared.speaker: narrator
cqb_fold_declared.text: Someone calls fold — surrender the firing position, give the enemy the angle. It costs: your crew loses an attack, the hostiles gain one. But it keeps bodies behind hard cover. The math is ugly. The math is the math.

cqb_height_advantage.speaker: narrator
cqb_height_advantage.text: The nav officer spots a catwalk overhead — two metres up, runs the length of the engagement zone. Height modifies everything: cover thickens, shots land cleaner. Climbing costs a turn. Staying flat costs nothing but accuracy.

cqb_end_won_pyrrhic.speaker: narrator
cqb_end_won_pyrrhic.text: You won. You won, and the taste of it is ash. Half your ammo is gone, the corridor is wrecked, and the medic is working on two crewmates who won't walk right for a week. The bond holds — barely. The ledger will show the cost.

cqb_end_negotiated_exit.speaker: narrator
cqb_end_negotiated_exit.text: Neither side could press the advantage. A voice from behind enemy cover called a corridor ceasefire — three minutes to evacuate. You took it. They took it. The gate stays contested. The run continues with less ammo and a strange respect.

cqb_fold_sacrifice.speaker: narrator
cqb_fold_sacrifice.text: The fold wasn't tactical — it was survival. One crewmate held the angle while the rest fell back. The angle held. The crewmate is behind the line, barely. This is what fold costs when there's no good choice.

choices.label: Pull them back. No one stays.
Hold the new line. They knew.
